mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.16 at t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) during sustained near-zero rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is elevated but begins declining by more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals labor market healing that precedes broader economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp over trailing 3 months while level remains above 8%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.7 at t-1mo, elevated but off cycle peak
regime_hint: labor market recovery from high-unemployment trough
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward returns are negative or not distinguishable from unconditional baseline (p>0.05) across a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and residential investment demand strength that jointly signals expanding aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures the compounding demand signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=351418 at t-1mo, HOUST=581.0 at t-2mo
regime_hint: any regime with positive co-movement of consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce 20d forward returns exceeding the single-series baseline by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and supply-side output, which correlates with near-term corporate earnings strength and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum readings show Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery from recession, acceleration in nonfarm payroll growth signals strengthening labor income and consumption capacity, which supports sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this relationship is muted or absent in late-cycle expansions where labor market tightness raises wage-cost concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=131223 at t-1mo, UNRATE=9.7 at t-1mo consistent with early recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery from recession with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: PAYEMS acceleration fails to predict positive 60d forward returns (Rank IC<0.03) specifically during periods when UNRATE remains above 8%, invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation year-over-year accelerates above 3%, markets price in expectations of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
regime_hint: rising inflation regime prompting tightening expectations
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, 20d forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p>0.05) across a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising housing starts jointly indicate that cheap financing is successfully stimulating residential investment, a leading indicator of broader economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect requires both conditions jointly, as low rates alone without housing response indicate stalled transmission.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.16 at t-1mo, HOUST=581.0 at t-2mo
regime_hint: monetary easing with responsive housing sector
failure_condition: Joint low-FEDFUNDS-and-rising-HOUST states fail to produce 60d forward returns exceeding unconditional baseline by a significant margin (p>0.05) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth below a 1% annualized quarter-over-quarter pace signals emerging recession risk that is not yet fully priced by equity markets, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as growth disappointments materialize.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1%
failure_condition: Triggered GDPC1 slowdown episodes are followed by positive or flat 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.
